license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.9qgh5tw
title: RNA isolation of Pinctada fucata martensii
author: Xiong Xinwei

steps_raw.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes to permit complete dissociation of the nucleoproteins complex.
Add a steel ball to each tube and then flacker for 3 min by TissueLayer 2. The frequency is 26 (1/S). Repeat this step one time.
Centrifuge the lysate for 15 minutes at 12,000 × g at 4 ℃, then transfer the aqueous phase containing the RNA (~500 ul) to a new 1.5 mL tube.
Take 50-100 mg of tissue to 2.0 mL tube, then add 1 mL of TRIzol™ Reagent per 50–100 mg of tissue to the tube.
Add 200 ul Chloroform (4 ℃ pre-cooling), then securely cap the tube and shake for 1 min to mix it completely. Then incubate for 10 min at 4 ℃.
Add 0.5 mL of isopropanol to per 0.5 mL aqueous phase, then mix it gently.
Incubate 10 min at 4 ℃. Then centrifuge for 10 minutes at 12,000 × g at 4 ℃.
Remove the supernatant as much as possible. Centrifuge for 1 minute at 12,000 × g at 4 ℃ to remove the supernatant completely if necessary.
Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 75% ethanol per 1 mL of TRIzol™ Reagent used for lysis.
Centrifuge for 5 min at 12,000 × g at 4 ℃, then discard the supernatant. Repeat step 9 and step 10 for one time.
Air-dry the RNA pellet for 5 min at 4 ℃.
Resuspend the RNA pellet in 20–50 ul of RNase-free water.
Measuring the concentration of RNA by the instrument of SimpliNano and detecting RNA integrity using a 1% agarose gel.
